skill.name: red-team-tools
skill.description: A comprehensive skill for bug bounty hunting and red team reconnaissance. Provides proven methodologies and automated workflows for subdomain enumeration, live host discovery, vulnerability scanning, and XSS hunting using industry-standard tools like Amass, Subfinder, httpx, Nuclei, and ffuf.
skill.summary: Automated red team reconnaissance and bug bounty hunting workflow using industry-standard security tools
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill provides legitimate red team methodology and bug bounty hunting workflows. Static findings flagged shell commands and network access, but these are standard security testing patterns (Amass, Subfinder, Nuclei, httpx, ffuf) used by authorized security professionals. No malicious intent detected. All flagged patterns represent legitimate defensive security tooling.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Execution in Documentation
medium_findings.description: The skill contains bash commands using external security tools. This is expected behavior for a red team methodology skill - tools like amass, subfinder, httpx, nuclei, and ffuf are industry-standard for authorized security testing. No user input is directly executed; commands use hardcoded tool invocations.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All shell commands invoke legitimate security tools with hardcoded arguments. The skill is designed for authorized bug bounty and red team work, with explicit constraints to respect program scope and rate limits.

medium_findings.title: Network Access for Reconnaissance
medium_findings.description: The skill references network access to external services (Shodan API, BGP lookup, Wayback Machine) which are standard recon resources for security testing. URLs and API endpoints shown are legitimate recon targets.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network references are to standard security recon services used in authorized bug bounty programs. The skill includes constraints to respect program scope boundaries.

content.value_statement: Bug bounty hunters and penetration testers need efficient reconnaissance workflows to enumerate targets and discover vulnerabilities. This skill provides automated pipelines using industry-standard tools like Amass, Subfinder, httpx, Nuclei, and ffuf for comprehensive security testing.
actual_capabilities: Perform automated subdomain enumeration using Amass, Subfinder, and Assetfinder
Discover live hosts and fingerprint technologies with httpx and httprobe
Execute content discovery and directory fuzzing with ffuf
Run vulnerability scans using Nuclei with custom templates
Automate XSS hunting with Dalfox and paramspider
Collect historical URLs from Wayback Machine and enumerate API endpoints
limitations: Requires pre-installed security tools (Go, Python, Ruby environments)
Cannot bypass rate limiting or IP blocks enforced by targets
Manual verification needed for all findings before bug bounty reporting
API keys required for full functionality of some recon services

use_cases.title: Quick Subdomain Recon
use_cases.description: Rapidly enumerate subdomains and check which ones are live, useful for initial target assessment during bug bounty hunts.
use_cases.target_user: Bug bounty hunters needing fast initial reconnaissance

use_cases.title: Full Vulnerability Assessment
use_cases.description: Comprehensive scan from subdomain enumeration through technology fingerprinting to nuclei vulnerability scanning.
use_cases.target_user: Penetration testers conducting authorized security assessments

use_cases.title: XSS Hunting Pipeline
use_cases.description: Automated pipeline to discover parameters and test for XSS vulnerabilities using multiple techniques.
use_cases.target_user: Security researchers focused on web application vulnerabilities

prompt_templates.title: Quick Subdomain Scan
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a quick subdomain enumeration for [TARGET_DOMAIN] using subfinder and check which hosts are live with httpx. Output the results to a file.
prompt_templates.scenario: Fast initial reconnaissance on a bug bounty target

prompt_templates.title: Full Recon Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Execute a complete reconnaissance workflow for [TARGET_DOMAIN]: 1) Run amass passive enum, 2) Use subfinder for additional subdomains, 3) Check live hosts with httprobe, 4) Run nuclei vulnerability scan on live hosts.

prompt_templates.title: XSS Vulnerability Hunt
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up an XSS hunting pipeline for [TARGET_DOMAIN]: 1) Use waybackurls to collect URLs, 2) Extract parameters, 3) Test with dalfox, 4) Verify findings with curl.

prompt_templates.title: API Endpoint Discovery
prompt_templates.prompt: Enumerate API endpoints for [TARGET_DOMAIN] using ffuf with common API wordlists. Test for both v1 and v2 API versions and check for hidden HTTP methods.

output_examples.output: Subdomain enumeration results showing live hosts with HTTP titles and status codes for quick prioritization
CVE vulnerability scan results with severity ratings, matched templates, and affected endpoints

best_practices: Always respect bug bounty program scope and rules before testing any target
Use rate limiting and appropriate concurrency settings to avoid triggering blocks
Verify all findings manually before submitting bug bounty reports to reduce duplicates
anti_patterns: Running automated tools without understanding what each command does
Ignoring program scope boundaries and testing out-of-scope targets
Submitting findings without manual verification, creating noise for program triage teams

faq.question: What tools need to be installed before using this skill?
faq.answer: This skill assumes you have Go, Python, and Ruby installed. Key tools include Amass, Subfinder, httpx, Nuclei, ffuf, Dalfox, and waybackurls. Most can be installed via go install or apt.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use on any target?
faq.answer: No. Only use this skill on targets where you have explicit authorization. Always check bug bounty program scope and rules before running any reconnaissance.

faq.question: How do I avoid getting rate limited or blocked?
faq.answer: Use the built-in rate limiting features of tools, reduce concurrency settings, and consider using proxy rotation for extended scans. The skill includes recommendations for these settings.

faq.question: Can this skill find all vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: No automated tool can find all vulnerabilities. This skill provides efficient reconnaissance and automated scanning, but manual testing is still required for comprehensive assessments.

faq.question: Do I need API keys for full functionality?
faq.answer: Some features like Shodan and Censys integration require API keys. Many recon techniques work without keys, but paid services provide additional coverage.

faq.question: How do I verify findings before reporting?
faq.answer: Manually reproduce each finding by visiting the affected endpoint. Use curl or browser developer tools to confirm the vulnerability exists and document steps to reproduce.
